protocol.question: Do persisted affective state and AFT-style retrieval improve recall on scripted multi-session scenarios compared with simpler baselines?

naive_cosine.notes: Semantic-only cosine retrieval with the same deterministic embedder.

recency.notes: Recency-only control with no semantic or affective ranking.

interpretation_guardrails: This is a controlled replay benchmark, not a production agent evaluation.
top1_accuracy is the headline metric; hit@k is secondary support.
Queries with candidate_count <= top_k are rejected as trivial by dataset validation.
Challenge-type aggregates are reported so localized AFT gains or failures are visible.
Only AFT models persisted affective state in this protocol.
Baselines remain useful controls for memory carry-over without affective state.
Results do not establish general downstream superiority or human-like memory.
